title: ulmus macrocarpa var. macrophylla
intro: Ulmus macrocarpa var. macrophylla, also known as the Rocky Mountain Elm, is a deciduous tree native to the western United States and Canada. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be beneficial in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Ulmus macrocarpa var. macrophylla include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate skin conditions, and support the immune system. Ulmus macrocarpa var. macrophylla is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and its extracts can also be used in topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
